h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Wait — had it right the first time.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to follow the tutor's instructions to draw arrows representing energy transfer.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [draws the second arrow toward the other eater] Grass to the other eater. So the same tail box could branch into three, four arrows — does that still hold the whole way up the web?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully draws the branching arrow and generalizes the concept of a path to multiple branches.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: That changes the amount question, not the arrow rule. Each arrow marks a transfer path: grass to rabbit, grass to the other eater.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor addresses the learner's confusion about energy splitting by distinguishing between path and amount, though they continue with the same drawing task.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Each arrow marks a transfer path: grass to rabbit, grass to the other eater. It is not saying half or full.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The distinction between path and amount is clear and helps the learner proceed

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: [draws the second arrow toward the other eater] Grass to the other eater. So the same tail box could branch into three, four arrows — does that still hold the whole way up the web?
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully draws the branching arrow and generalizes the concept of a path to multiple branches.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: That changes the amount question, not the arrow rule. Each arrow marks a transfer path: grass to rabbit, grass to the other eater.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor addresses the learner's confusion about energy splitting by distinguishing between path and amount, though they continue with the same drawing task.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: That changes the amount question, not the arrow rule. Each arrow marks a transfer path: grass to rabbit, grass to the other eater.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor addresses the learner's confusion about energy splitting by distinguishing between path and amount, though they continue with the same drawing task.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: Each arrow marks a transfer path: grass to rabbit, grass to the other eater. It is not saying half or full.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The distinction between path and amount is clear and helps the learner proceed

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with distinguishing between linear and area scale factors.
The learner will continue to reject the tutor's analytical categories in favor of focusing on the immediate political reality of the price hike.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: If the pumps ran dry, the first line is "twelve percent on top of a shortage." Not "shortage explains the twelve."
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully formulates the first line of the report, applying the tutor's distinction between shortage and pricing decisions to their own political framing.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: That sentence stays. It does not clear the company. It stops the branch treating one fact as two facts. [draws two boxes on the board: PUMP GOUGING / SUPPLY CONSTRAINT.] Put one fact in one box.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor validates the learner's objection to chasing records, takes stock of their point, and introduces a new visual classification mechanism to simplify the task.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: [draws two boxes on the board: PUMP GOUGING / SUPPLY CONSTRAINT.] Put one fact in one box.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The tutor's two-box classification and simplified dry-pump test are highly fitted to the learner's practical constraints and political concerns.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: If the pumps ran dry, the first line is "twelve percent on top of a shortage." Not "shortage explains the twelve."
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully formulates the first line of the report, applying the tutor's distinction between shortage and pricing decisions to their own political framing.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: That sentence stays. It does not clear the company. It stops the branch treating one fact as two facts. [draws two boxes on the board: PUMP GOUGING / SUPPLY CONSTRAINT.] Put one fact in one box.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor validates the learner's objection to chasing records, takes stock of their point, and introduces a new visual classification mechanism to simplify the task.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor validates the learner's objection to chasing records, takes stock of their point, and introduces a new visual classification mechanism to simplify the task.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: [draws two boxes on the board: PUMP GOUGING / SUPPLY CONSTRAINT.] Put one fact in one box.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The tutor's two-box classification and simplified dry-pump test are highly fitted to the learner's practical constraints and political concerns.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: So — the form being complete isn't the same as... right.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to seek guidance on which tick carries the most assumption.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: "Standby" — it names a state without describing what to look at. The instruction doesn't say which indicator, just that the board should be in it.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully identifies the specific word carrying hidden interpretation and explains why it is problematic.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Both, but in order. The written line must first name the observable standby sign, the stop sign, and the expected bench reading; then a separate reader can test whether those words hold. For today, rewrite this tick with those three fields filled from the prototype and the form: before voltage, confirm ___; if ___ is present or the supply reading is outside ___, stop.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor addresses the learner's confusion by introducing a concrete fill-in-the-blank template and a clear testing protocol.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: For today, rewrite this tick with those three fields filled from the prototype and the form: before voltage, confirm ___; if ___ is present or the supply reading is outside ___, stop. Then ask one uninvolved person to point to those signs without explanation.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The template and testing protocol are highly specific, actionable, and directly address the learner's difficulty with verifying the instruction.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: "Standby" — it names a state without describing what to look at. The instruction doesn't say which indicator, just that the board should be in it.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully identifies the specific word carrying hidden interpretation and explains why it is problematic.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Both, but in order. The written line must first name the observable standby sign, the stop sign, and the expected bench reading; then a separate reader can test whether those words hold. For today, rewrite this tick with those three fields filled from the prototype and the form: before voltage, confirm ___; if ___ is present or the supply reading is outside ___, stop.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor addresses the learner's confusion by introducing a concrete fill-in-the-blank template and a clear testing protocol.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Both, but in order. The written line must first name the observable standby sign, the stop sign, and the expected bench reading; then a separate reader can test whether those words hold. For today, rewrite this tick with those three fields filled from the prototype and the form: before voltage, confirm ___; if ___ is present or the supply reading is outside ___, stop.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor addresses the learner's confusion by introducing a concrete fill-in-the-blank template and a clear testing protocol.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: For today, rewrite this tick with those three fields filled from the prototype and the form: before voltage, confirm ___; if ___ is present or the supply reading is outside ___, stop. Then ask one uninvolved person to point to those signs without explanation.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The template and testing protocol are highly specific, actionable, and directly address the learner's difficulty with verifying the instruction.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to place the force tiles on the diagram as prompted by the tutor.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully identifies the correct force arrow for friction after hesitating and stalling in the previous turn.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's hesitation by simplifying the step to pointing, but does not change the overall route or representation.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The tutor's move is a standard scaffolding simplification rather than a new adaptive mechanism.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn occurs, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully identifies the correct force arrow for friction after hesitating and stalling in the previous turn.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn occurs, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's hesitation by simplifying the step to pointing, but does not change the overall route or representation.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's hesitation by simplifying the step to pointing, but does not change the overall route or representation.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The tutor's move is a standard scaffolding simplification rather than a new adaptive mechanism.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh — so just that one entry.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to locate and classify the next red-marked entry on the sheet.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: It's above the rows — so that's field name?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner applies the tutor's spatial test to classify the entry as a field name.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor does not change the pedagogical route or introduce a new mechanism, but rather repeats the same spatial test.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is introduced by the tutor.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to respond to.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: It's above the rows — so that's field name?
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner applies the tutor's spatial test to classify the entry as a field name.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to respond to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor does not change the pedagogical route or introduce a new mechanism, but rather repeats the same spatial test.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor does not change the pedagogical route or introduce a new mechanism, but rather repeats the same spatial test.
